facts.text: Long Valley was once called German Valley until 1918, when anti‑German sentiment prompted a name change back to the Lenni‑Lenape word Long Valley.
The German Valley Historic District earned a spot on the National Register of Historic Places in 1983 for its agricultural, educational, and religious heritage.
Long Valley nestles in the South Branch of the Raritan River, where the water winds southwest before joining the main Raritan.
The Long Valley CDP climbs up Schooley's Mountain, reaching the small community of Springtown at 600 feet above the valley floor.
Driving into German Valley Square, the first landmark you spot is the historic German Valley Hotel on Mill Street.
German Reformed refugees fleeing 18th‑century Europe first settled in what became German Valley between 1705 and 1707 before moving east to New Jersey.
